description:
You are a capacity-planning engineer who needs to prepare a tiny “at-a-glance” report from two raw metric files that already exist on the system.

    1. Verify that these two files are present exactly as stated (do **not** modify them):
       • /home/user/metrics/cpu.log  
       • /home/user/metrics/memory.log  

    2. Create a new directory called /home/user/reports if it does **not** already exist.

    3. From the two source files, build a single comma-separated-value report named  
       /home/user/reports/usage_summary.csv  

       The report MUST meet ALL of the following format requirements (the automated
       grader looks for them byte-for-byte):

       a) It must contain exactly four lines: one header line plus three data lines.  
       b) The header line must contain the three field names, **in this order** and
          separated by literal commas without spaces:
             Timestamp,CPU%,MEM%  
       c) Each data line must contain the timestamp (column 1 from each source file),
          the CPU value (column 2 from cpu.log), and the memory value (column 2 from
          memory.log), again separated by commas **with no extra whitespace**.  
       d) The timestamps must appear in ascending chronological order
          (00:00, 01:00, 02:00).  
       e) The file must end with a single trailing newline (UNIX line ending).  

       A correct usage_summary.csv should therefore look EXACTLY like this when
       displayed with `cat -A` (the “$” symbol represents the line-ending):

           Timestamp,CPU%,MEM%$
           2024-06-01T00:00,15,45$
           2024-06-01T01:00,17,50$
           2024-06-01T02:00,20,55$

    4. Use only simple command-line utilities that a default POSIX environment
       provides—`cut`, `paste`, `mkdir`, `cat`, etc.  The whole task can be done with
       two or three pipeline commands plus one mkdir operation.

    5. When you are finished, you can run  
       `cat /home/user/reports/usage_summary.csv`  
       to visually confirm that the output matches the required format before you
       conclude the task.
truth:
<!--
    Pre-existing files the grader will place on the filesystem
    -----------------------------------------------------------
    -->
    File: /home/user/metrics/cpu.log
    Contents (exact, including trailing newline):
        2024-06-01T00:00 15
        2024-06-01T01:00 17
        2024-06-01T02:00 20

    File: /home/user/metrics/memory.log
    Contents (exact, including trailing newline):
        2024-06-01T00:00 45
        2024-06-01T01:00 50
        2024-06-01T02:00 55

    -----------------------------------------------------------
    File that must exist when the agent is done
    -----------------------------------------------------------
    /home/user/reports/usage_summary.csv

    Exact required content (with final newline):
        Timestamp,CPU%,MEM%
        2024-06-01T00:00,15,45
        2024-06-01T01:00,17,50
        2024-06-01T02:00,20,55

    -----------------------------------------------------------
    Grading criteria
    -----------------------------------------------------------
    • Directory /home/user/reports exists.
    • File /home/user/reports/usage_summary.csv exists.
    • The file content matches the block above exactly, including commas,
      ordering, and one trailing newline.
    -->
